title: prunus serrulata f. pubifolia
intro: Prunus serrulata f. pubifolia, also known as the Japanese flowering cherry or pubescent Japanese cherry, is a variant of the flowering cherry tree (Prunus serrulata) characterized by its hairy young leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine in East Asia, particularly in China and Japan, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Prunus serrulata f. pubifolia include reducing fever, alleviating coughs and sore throats, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
